claimText: NavIC provides coverage over the Indian landmass and a region extending up to 1500 km beyond it.
evidenceExcerpt: NavIC coverage extends up to 1500 km beyond the Indian landmass.
examUse: Statement-based question on NavIC's coverage area.
distractorHooks: Coverage up to 5000 km beyond India
Coverage restricted only to the Indian landmass

claimText: The NavIC constellation consists of 7 satellites, with 3 in geostationary orbit and 4 in inclined geosynchronous orbit.
evidenceExcerpt: The constellation consists of 7 satellites: 3 in geostationary orbit and 4 in inclined geosynchronous orbit.

claimText: NavIC operates using dual-band signals, specifically L5 and S-band, with the L5 signal encrypted for military use.
evidenceExcerpt: NavIC uses dual-band signals (L5 and S-band), with the L5 signal encrypted for military use.
examUse: Statement-based question on signal bands and encryption.

claimText: NavIC offers Standard Position Service (SPS) for civilian users and Restricted Service (RS) for strategic users.
evidenceExcerpt: It offers Standard Position Service (SPS) for civilians and Restricted Service (RS) for strategic users.
distractorHooks: SPS for military and RS for civilians

claimText: Global navigation systems equivalent to NavIC include GLONASS (Russia), Galileo (EU), BeiDou (China), and QZSS (Japan).
evidenceExcerpt: Global equivalents include GLONASS (Russia), Galileo (EU), BeiDou (China), and QZSS (Japan).
examUse: Correct-pair question matching navigation systems to their respective countries or regions.
